task: R6-B — Healthcare practice state privacy add-on (Washington RCW 70.02)
disclaimer: Operational compliance research — not legal advice. RCW 70.02 may impose requirements beyond HIPAA.
policy: Public statute sources only; gaps explicit.

medical_records_privacy.label: RCW 70.02 — Health Care Information Access and Disclosure

cited_facts.text: Except as authorized elsewhere in chapter 70.02, a health care provider may not disclose health care information about a patient without the patient's written authorization.
A valid disclosure authorization must be in writing, dated, signed, identify the information and recipients, identify the provider, identify the patient, and contain an expiration date or event.
Authorizations permitting disclosure to a financial institution or employer for non-payment purposes expire one year after signing unless renewed.
A health care provider shall effect reasonable safeguards for the security of all health care information it maintains.

Persons receiving health care information for health care operations may not use or disclose it in a manner that would violate chapter 70.02 if performed by the provider; providers may terminate contracts if a pattern of violation is not cured.
